title: Mohamed Cherkaoui: Invisible Codes: Essays on Generative Mechanisms * Mohamed Cherkaoui: Good Intentions: Max Weber and the Paradox of Unintended Consequences
abstract: Invisible Codes and Good Intentions are published in a new series from Bardwell Press under the title GEMAS Studies in Social Analysis. GEMAS, the acronym for Groupe d’Etude des Méthodes de l’Analyse Sociologique, is a group of sociologists at CNRS interested in sociological theory, method and application to real-world problems. The admirable ambition of the series editors is to make the work of this group available to the English-reading audience. The author of both books, Professor Mohamed Cherkaoui, is research director at CNRS with a distinguished scholarly career in the tradition of Raymond Boudon and James Coleman. As far as I can judge, Peter Hamilton has done a very good job in translating Cherkaoui from French. Both volumes are treatises on social mechanisms. They are overall thoughtful, critical, and clearly written, and should be of interest to the growing number of sociologists concerned with the task of strengthening the theoretical...
